May I ask why you're tired of rick and Morty?

​@WilliamsSteve-h2cThe series feels like its overstayed its welcome. After Rick Prime died and Evil Morty returned I feel like that's where the story should end. 

A big problem I've had with the show is the main characters don't have well developed lives outside the family which limits there development. 

Its like the opposite problem with the Loud House where the main characters now spend to much time away from there family just to hang out with characters who are not well defined.

​@Junior-t4d9r  I get where you're coming from but I don't think it would be a good idea to end the show at that point because alot of things leading up to the confrontation with Rick prime was hinting that the show wouldn't end there, Even when rick killed prime he felt empty, unfulfilled like it wasn't over. Rick still has alot of mistakes to pay for and alot of character development to undergo why end the show if his character arc isn't even over yet? 


Now this I don't get, a big part of the show is repeatedly telling us that the family are all losers and terrible people, Beth is too crazy and messed up to actually develop bonds with anyone, jerry is too insecure about everything to do anything, summer really is just a shitbag and Morty is too busy with Rick and his adventures that he's wasting any chance of meeting like-minded people and developing healthy relationships, Rick is too toxic that he eventually pushes everybody in his life away from him. I don't agree that it limits their development since it's part of their development, they were initially so dysfunctional as a family that they all felt alone and unseen, now they're learning how to be a family and relying on eachother and supporting one another that's development. Rick also repeatedly makes it a point that everyone is alone in the universe, nothing matters, nothing is real but as time goes on he realises that he's not alone he has a family and that family matters alot to him and it is very much real. Their lives suck to the point where they only have eachother why waste that and look for meaningless bonds when you can preserve the only bond you have. All in all I get what you mean but I don't feel like it's overstayed it's welcome,  rick and Morty as a show allows them to explore multiple themes and different concepts and even create new concepts so I do think the show still has alot of untapped potential, it's mainly just the Justin roiland exit and the inconsistency of the writing that has made it lose its original charm but season 8 has been looking really good
